items.title: Fix layout issues for head-safe images and text stacking in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update addresses two critical layout bugs affecting the rendering of bios: head cropping and text overlap. Key changes include enforcing a consistent `object-position` to prevent head cropping and restructuring text into a flex column to eliminate overlap. These adjustments enhance the visual integrity of the rendered content, ensuring a better user experience.

items.title: Rename MCP Tools to Match Prefix in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors all client-exposed identifiers in the AceDataCloud MCP, changing the prefix from 'platform_' to 'acedatacloud_'. This is a purely mechanical rename with no changes to functionality, ensuring consistency with the server/package naming. Documentation and tests have been updated accordingly to reflect these changes.

items.title: Launch Hosted MCP for AceDataCloud in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: The platform management MCP is now live at https://mcp.acedata.cloud/mcp, providing a centralized endpoint for the AceDataCloud services. Key updates include the addition of deployment configurations, service definitions, and automated workflows for publishing to PyPI and GitHub. This enhancement improves accessibility and operational efficiency for managing cloud services.

items.title: Enhance message events with canonical sender_id in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the canonical sender_id to the message.new WebSocket payloads and ensures it is persisted through the database bridge. Key files modified include db_bridge.py and wechat_frida_service.py, with successful validation through compileall and targeted pytest. The adversarial review concluded with a clean verdict.

items.title: Update hosted endpoint for AceDataCloud in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update changes the hosted endpoint for the unified AceDataCloud platform-management MCP to the new subdomain https://mcp.acedata.cloud/mcp. The per-service MCPs retain their prefixes, ensuring continuity for users. Documentation has been updated accordingly in the README and MCP servers table.

items.title: Update hosted endpoint for AceDataCloud MCP in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: The hosted endpoint for the `acedatacloud` MCP server has been updated to the new bare subdomain `https://mcp.acedata.cloud/mcp`, replacing the previous URL. This change is reflected in the documentation, specifically in the `acedatacloud/README.md` file. No code or tests were affected by this update, ensuring a smooth transition.

items.title: Updated Install Guide for AceDataExt in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The installation guide for the Ace Data Cloud browser extension has been updated to link to the newly verified v0.4.2 package, which resolves previous runtime errors. This change ensures users can successfully download and use the extension without issues. The updated URL has been applied across all 18 language versions of the documentation.

items.title: Streamline UIA Functionality in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update significantly refactors the UI Automation (UIA) functionality by consolidating the reading and syncing processes to utilize only the WeChat database and Frida for contact management. Key changes include the removal of redundant UIA methods and scripts, leading to a cleaner codebase with a net change of +91/-5604 across 46 files. The overall impact enhances performance and maintainability while ensuring all essential features remain intact.

items.title: Fix timeline key binding to prevent blank renders in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the issue of blank renders in Maestro by ensuring the timeline key is derived from the DOM rather than hardcoded. Key changes include modifying the authoring rules and brand kit components to enforce the requirement of a root element with a `data-composition-id`. This fix enhances the reliability of video rendering by preventing mismatches that lead to empty frames.

items.title: Add AceData Brand Icons for Desktop Apps in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces the AceData brand icons for both macOS and Windows desktop applications, replacing the default Electron atom icon. The new icons are included in the build directory and configured in the electron-builder settings, ensuring they are used for the DMG and NSIS installers. Additionally, a script has been added to regenerate these icons from the existing iOS brand icon, streamlining future updates.

items.title: Add missing storage permission for cookie keep-alive in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes a TypeError in the background service worker by adding the missing `storage` permission to the manifest. The version has been bumped to 0.4.2, ensuring that the `browser.storage.local` API can be utilized without errors. The changes have been verified with a rebuilt distribution.

items.title: Revamped WeChat Trust Policy for Enhanced Access Control in Wisdom Channel 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new v3 `access.json` schema, replacing the legacy allowlist with improved role management and permissions. Key features include role-level tool access, enhanced security measures for interactive channels, and a local CLI for policy edits. The changes aim to streamline access control and bolster security for the WeChat channel.

items.title: Updated Installation Guide for ACE Extension in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The installation documentation for the ACE extension has been updated across all 18 locales to point to the new icon-fixed v0.4.1 package. This change ensures that users download the correct version, which includes the manifest `icons` field for improved logo rendering in Chrome. The update involved a straightforward URL swap, verified to function correctly.

items.title: Fix extension logo rendering and version bump in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the issue of the extension displaying a gray placeholder logo by adding a top-level `icons` field in the manifest. The `action.default_icon` has also been modified to include a sized map for better icon display. Additionally, the version has been bumped from 0.4 to 0.4.1, ensuring users receive the updated package with the correct logo.

items.title: Enhance Short Drama Experience in Platform Service 🎬
items.summary: This update introduces version 1.1 of the short-drama skill, allowing for engaging narratives of approximately 2 minutes with multiple characters and scenes. Key improvements include extended shot durations, a structured plot arc, and enhanced character consistency through defined character profiles. The architecture documentation has also been updated to reflect these changes, ensuring clarity in the new capabilities.

items.title: Update ACE Extension Install Guide to v0.4 in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The ACE browser-extension installation documentation has been updated to point to the new v0.4 package. This version introduces periodic cookie keep-alive functionality, ensuring that stored snapshots remain fresh. The update affects all 18 locales, with the new download link verified for correctness.

items.title: Streamline OpenAI Checker with Mongo-Only Config in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update transitions the OpenAI checker to exclusively use MongoDB for configuration, removing all JSON config files and related functionalities. Key changes include the deletion of multiple config files and the introduction of tests to ensure no config file reads occur. This streamlining enhances data consistency by establishing Mongo as the sole source of truth for provider and capability data.

items.title: Add AceDataCloud BYOC Connector in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a first-party AceDataCloud connector to the connector directory, allowing users to manage their accounts using a Platform Token. The token is securely stored and injected as an environment variable for seamless integration with the `acedatacloud` skill and the MCP server. This addition completes the necessary components for effective account management.

items.title: Streamlined Codebase and Removed Redundant Listeners in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates the Wisdom codebase by removing duplicate implementations and redundant listener chains. Key changes include the deletion of unused scripts and endpoints, ensuring that only the most efficient code remains. The impact is a cleaner, more maintainable codebase that enhances performance and reduces complexity.

items.title: Clean Up Maestro's Skill Allowlist in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the now-deleted `acedatacloud-api` skill from Maestro's skill allowlist and associated documentation. The change streamlines the skill set by eliminating redundancy, as per-service skills already cover their API usage. This cleanup enhances the clarity and functionality of the Maestro system.

items.title: Unify Account Management with New MCP Server in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: The new `mcp-acedatacloud` server integrates account management and documentation tools for the AceDataCloud platform. It combines previous public documentation and introduces 29 tools across two access tiers, enhancing user experience. Key features include optional token usage and a consistent naming convention for management tools, ensuring a streamlined interface for users.

items.title: Streamline Account Management with New AceDataCloud Skill in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the new `acedatacloud` skill, which consolidates account management functionalities for the AceDataCloud platform. The redundant `acedatacloud-api` skill has been removed, enhancing clarity and usability. Key features include account balance checks, API key management, and access to public services without authentication, all through a streamlined CLI interface.

items.title: Enhance Subtitle Quality and Text Rendering in Platform Service 🎬
items.summary: This update addresses critical quality issues identified during the testing of a short drama. Key changes include the implementation of ASS subtitle standards to prevent oversized captions and text cutoff, as well as new rules prohibiting model-rendered text in frames to avoid rendering issues with Chinese characters. These improvements aim to enhance the overall viewing experience.

items.title: Enhance asset handling for skills in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a content-addressed pull-through mechanism for skill bundles in the sandbox, addressing issues with multi-replica environments. Each pod can now pull asset bundles on demand using their content hash, eliminating reliance on sticky sessions and shared mutable storage. The changes ensure that any pod can serve any session without encountering missing files, significantly improving reliability.

items.title: Enhance Admin Controls for Better UX in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the admin interface by preventing the clipping of the 'Skip checks' control and adjusting the layout for better visibility of Chinese labels. It also implements a silent refresh mechanism to maintain immediate row state without a full model-list refresh, enhancing user experience during provider toggles. All validations have passed successfully.

items.title: Enhance Photo Verification for Identity and Focus in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the photo verification process by ensuring that the main subject of an image is not only identified correctly but also in clear focus. Changes were made to the verification steps in the SKILL.md and provider-map.md files, enhancing the criteria for confirming identities across various categories, including people, products, and places. This fix aims to reduce instances of incorrect image associations, thereby improving overall fidelity.

items.title: Introducing Short-Drama Skill for Maestro in Platform Service 🎬
items.summary: This update adds a new `short-drama` skill to Maestro, enabling agents to create vertical AI short dramas from scripts. The workflow bypasses HyperFrames, utilizing a per-shot image-to-video approach combined with ffmpeg for seamless video assembly. Key changes include new data contracts and a comprehensive director's recipe for producing engaging short stories.

items.title: Enhance Admin Console UI for Better Spacing in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the UI of the admin backend console by adjusting the spacing of the 'Add provider' button and separating the capability drawer switches from the submit action. These changes prevent overcrowding and ensure a more compact design for the Add Provider drawer. All validations have passed successfully, confirming the stability of the modifications.

items.title: Revamp Maestro with Fresh Docs and Brand Kit in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the Maestro video service by fixing documentation drift, externalizing prompts, and streamlining skill rules. Key changes include a revised README, a new design system with a brand kit, and the separation of prompt texts from Python code for better maintainability. These improvements aim to ensure consistency and clarity in video production workflows.

items.title: Streamlining Wisdom: One Implementation Per Feature in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive plan to consolidate the Wisdom service by retaining only the best implementation for each feature, eliminating redundancy across Frida, WeChat DB, and UIA. Key changes include the deletion of outdated code paths and a structured approach to ensure all remaining imports are valid. The plan has undergone three rounds of rigorous peer review to address potential issues and ensure a smooth transition.

items.title: Introducing the Short-Drama Workflow for Maestro in Index 🎬
items.summary: This update presents a new `short-drama` workflow designed for Maestro, integrating research from seven open-source short-drama tools. Key features include a seven-stage pipeline for script-to-video transformation, ensuring character consistency and effective scene transitions. The implementation leverages existing technologies like seedance 2.0 while addressing narrative structuring and audio synchronization.

items.title: Enhance Image URL Handling in Skills 📸
items.summary: This update improves the handling of Zhihu's image URLs by converting draft-scoped signed URLs into durable public formats. The changes ensure that images stored in articles remain accessible and stable, preventing issues with expired authentication keys. Additionally, the update includes robust checks to avoid mishandling non-Zhihu URLs.

items.title: Enhance Image Quality and Layout in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces critical fixes to the image handling process, ensuring that watermarked photos are rejected and broken image placeholders are eliminated. The layout now features a cohesive dark gradient scrim applied only behind text, enhancing readability while maintaining the integrity of the images. These changes significantly improve the visual quality and user experience in the Platform Service.

items.title: Enhance cookie keep-alive for ACE extension in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new endpoints for the ACE browser extension to periodically refresh cookie credentials for connected BYOC cookie connectors. The changes ensure that the stored cookie snapshot remains current, preventing failures due to session invalidation on the server side. Key safety features have been implemented to protect user data and maintain connection integrity.

items.title: Enhance Cookie Management for ACE Extension in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a periodic cookie keep-alive mechanism for the ACE browser extension, ensuring that users' connected cookie connectors remain fresh. A new `chrome.alarms` job runs every 30 minutes to refresh cookies, improving the user experience by maintaining live sessions. Key modifications include updates to `background.ts`, `content_script.tsx`, and `manifest.json` to support this functionality.

items.title: Enhance Upstream Traffic Display in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update explicitly shows zero requests in the upstream dashboard when there is no traffic in the last 24 hours. Additionally, the success rate and p95 metrics will remain blank under these conditions. The changes have been validated with all tests passing successfully.

items.title: Docs Sync Confirmed for aichat CLI in Clis 🚀
items.summary: The documentation for the `aichat` CLI has been reviewed to ensure alignment with the latest OpenAPI definitions. No changes were necessary as the existing CLI already matches the updated specifications, confirming that all command surfaces and model enums are in parity with the documentation.

items.title: Streamline OpenAI Checker Configs in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update cleans up the OpenAI checker configuration files by removing unnecessary routing control fields, ensuring they only bootstrap missing accounts and carry essential secret/check hints. Key changes include the removal of `enabled`, `rank`, and `skip_check` fields from the JSON files while preserving capability model lists and check metadata. The modifications enhance the integrity of the configuration by preventing overrides of operator-controlled fields.

items.title: Enhance Admin Console UX in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the admin console interface by adjusting filter controls, improving spacing, and clarifying provider health counts. It also updates language for better understanding and adds a provider-level detection history entry, along with displaying traffic statistics when available. These changes aim to enhance user experience and functionality in the admin console.

items.title: Enhance Admin Dashboard with Traffic and History in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces crucial enhancements to the admin upstream dashboard, including 24-hour LLM traffic sample statistics and a new provider-level checker history endpoint. The changes optimize data retrieval by implementing a bounded recent-row sample, ensuring efficient performance while providing comprehensive insights into API usage. Validation tests confirm the stability and reliability of the new features.

items.title: Enhance Async Authorization Documentation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update documents the async and unattended authorization fields for the `/aichat2/conversations` API, including `async`, `callback_url`, and others. A new section in Chinese has been added to guide users on webhook and background alerting scenarios. These changes enable API callers to pre-authorize Skills and MCP servers for unattended execution, improving usability.

items.title: Enhance Unattended Authorization for Direct API Calls in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ability for direct `/aichat2/conversations` API callers to pass unattended authorization allowlists as top-level request fields. The new fields `allowed_skills` and `allowed_mcp_servers` serve as convenient aliases, enhancing the flexibility of API interactions without the need for scheduled tasks. Additionally, existing nested `unattended_policy` functionality remains intact and merges seamlessly with the new aliases.

items.title: Enhance Image Handling for Zhihu Publishing in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses the issue of missing images when publishing to Zhihu by implementing a robust image re-hosting solution. The new functionality allows for both direct uploads to Zhihu's CDN and fallback options for external images, ensuring that all images are preserved in published articles. Additionally, the changes include improved reporting on image processing during the publishing workflow.

items.title: Refactor Unattended Confirmation Guard in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update extracts the unattended scheduled-task confirmation guard into a reusable helper, enhancing code maintainability. The new guard is added in `skills/_shared/unattended.py` and integrated into the existing `personal-wechat` Skill, ensuring it functions correctly in sandbox packaging. Additionally, the `personal_wechat.py` script has been updated to utilize this shared guard, streamlining the codebase.

items.title: Enhance MCP Server Authorization for Scheduled Tasks in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ability to select authorized MCP servers for unattended scheduled tasks in the Nexior chat interface. Key changes include the addition of a multi-select option for MCP servers alongside existing Skill selectors, and updates to internationalization for various languages. These enhancements aim to streamline the authorization process for scheduled tasks, improving user experience and functionality.

items.title: Enhance MCP Server Authorization for Scheduled Tasks in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update expands the unattended authorization for scheduled tasks to include both Skills and MCP servers. Key changes include the addition of `allowed_mcp_servers` to the unattended policy, ensuring that only pre-authorized MCP servers can be loaded during unattended runs. This enhancement improves security by preventing arbitrary MCP server loads and maintains backward compatibility with existing policies.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Helper for Task Management in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the Personal WeChat helper by enabling it to handle queued search and confirmed send responses effectively. It introduces task polling via the API while ensuring the API token remains secure in the Authorization header. Additionally, documentation clarifies that the helper waits for queued UI operations before displaying final results.

items.title: Enhance Image Quality and Consistency in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces a cohesive image treatment for maestro brand/bio videos, ensuring all photos maintain a uniform look through consistent CSS styling. Additionally, a new 'Image polish' section in the provider map allows for selective enhancements using gpt-image-2, while strictly adhering to subject safety guidelines. These changes aim to elevate the overall quality and professionalism of the visual content.

items.title: Enhance Personal WeChat UI Task Queue in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a FIFO UI task queue and a new polling API for Personal WeChat UI automation, significantly improving the handling of UI operations. Key operations like sending messages and syncing contacts have been converted to task responses, enhancing performance and reliability. The CLI and web UI have been updated accordingly to reflect these changes, ensuring a smoother user experience.

items.title: Revamp Scheduled Tasks Page for a Sleek Look in Nexior ✨
items.summary: The Scheduled Tasks page has undergone a significant redesign to enhance user experience. Key updates include a new page-tone background, task cards with hover effects, and client-side pagination for both tasks and run history. Additionally, the design features larger card radii and filled status tags for better visibility.

items.title: Boosted Render Speed with Script Optimization in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update significantly improves the rendering speed of Maestro by optimizing the narration script and utilizing all available CPU resources. Key changes include tighter script requirements to fit the requested duration and a new rendering rule that maximizes CPU usage without crashing Chromium. The result is a more efficient rendering process, reducing the time taken for video production.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI Request Logging in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the archiving of raw OpenAI request and response data to Tencent COS object storage, ensuring no data truncation. The implementation captures full payloads, including headers and bodies, while masking sensitive information. This change replaces the previous MongoDB approach, improving data integrity and security by requiring a dedicated private bucket for storage.

items.title: Enhance Admin Backend Controls in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update brings final polish to the admin backend controls, enhancing the user interface based on production feedback. Key changes include improved alignment of headings, a new icon mini-card style for summary cards, and streamlined provider management functionalities. Additionally, routing issues have been addressed to ensure model cards refresh correctly after edits.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI Checker Configuration in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures that the OpenAI checker retains the portal and Mongo provider configuration as the control source. Key changes include preventing the config JSON from overwriting capability membership and preserving existing Mongo capabilities. The admin portal now effectively manages provider enablement and behavior, enhancing overall stability.

items.title: Empower Admin with Upstream Provider CRUD APIs in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces CRUD APIs for managing upstream providers and their capabilities within the admin portal. Key features include the ability to create and delete provider accounts and capabilities, as well as update the provider's API key directly from the portal. These enhancements streamline provider management, addressing operator requests for comprehensive control over supplier and capability settings.

items.title: Revamp nanobanana's aspect-ratio selector for visual consistency in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update transforms the nanobanana aspect-ratio control from a plain text dropdown to a visually appealing preview-chip selector, aligning it with other scenarios. Key enhancements include a grid layout for seven aspect ratios, improved accessibility features, and the addition of an info tooltip. The functionality remains intact, preserving the ability to toggle the aspect ratio off without defaults.

items.title: Fix iOS App Store Link Display in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where the iOS App Store link on the release download page was displaying placeholder text. The GitHub Actions workflow now correctly derives the iOS URL from either a repo variable or a hardcoded constant, ensuring the link is accurate by default. This change enhances the user experience by providing direct access to the live app on the App Store.

items.title: Refine DigitalHuman Config Panel for Consistency in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update enhances the DigitalHuman config panel to align with the polished standards set by the Maestro and Kling scenarios. Key changes include updated section labels, the addition of InfoIcons for tooltips, and improved spacing for better visual rhythm. The refactor maintains existing functionality and ensures a cleaner, more consistent user experience across scenarios.

items.title: Add Todoist Connector to Public Directory in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the official Todoist remote MCP server to the public connector directory, allowing users to easily browse and install it from Studio. The integration includes OAuth authentication and supports various productivity features, enhancing the overall functionality for users managing tasks and projects. The connector has been thoroughly validated and is now live with a dedicated icon.

items.title: Enhance Personal WeChat with Unattended Scheduled Confirmation in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new unattended scheduled-task confirmation feature for the Personal WeChat Skill. It adds the `--unattended-confirm` flag to enable unattended sending under specific conditions while maintaining existing chat behaviors. Comprehensive documentation and validation tests have been included to ensure functionality and security.

items.title: Enhance Scheduled Tasks with Skill Pre-Authorization in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new UI for pre-authorizing selected Skills during unattended background runs of scheduled tasks. Key changes include the addition of `unattended_policy` and `retrieve_authorizable_skills` types, a multi-select feature for Skills in the task dialog, and comprehensive i18n support across all locales. A warning confirmation is now displayed before saving any Skill authorizations.

items.title: Enhance Skill Authorization for Scheduled Tasks in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new unattended skill-authorization policy for scheduled tasks in aichat2. Key changes include the ability to store and validate skill authorization, ensuring that only selected skills can be executed during unattended operations. The implementation also enhances the query engine and Bash sandbox environment to support these policies, improving security and functionality.

items.title: Introducing TickTick Connector for Seamless Integration in Backend 🚀
items.summary: We've added the 滴答清单 (TickTick) third-party MCP connector to our directory, marking a significant enhancement for productivity integrations. This connector utilizes the official remote MCP server, allowing for a smooth OAuth DCR installation without any code changes. The addition includes a new entry in the connectors.yaml file, complete with a unique identifier and dynamic client registration.

items.title: Enhance Async Support for Conversations in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces `async: true` support for the `POST /aichat2/conversations` endpoint, allowing for faster acknowledgment in webhook scenarios. When this option is enabled, the system queues the conversation immediately and responds with a `202 Accepted` status, while processing continues in the background. This change improves integration with Tencent CLS, enabling quicker responses and later delivery of AI-generated results.

items.title: Revamp Maestro Config Panel for Consistency in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update enhances the Maestro left config panel to align with the design of Kling and Midjourney panels. Key changes include the introduction of shared components like `PromptTextarea` and `InfoIcon` tooltips, which streamline the interface and improve user experience. Additionally, new visual selectors for aspect ratio and a compact layout for duration and quality settings have been implemented, ensuring a cleaner and more cohesive look.

items.title: Enhance API Pricing Table with Localized Remarks in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new optional 'remark' feature for cost rules in the API pricing table, providing localized explanations for model variants. The changes include updates to the Price component and models interface, ensuring that remarks are displayed correctly without compromising existing functionality. This enhancement improves user understanding of pricing parameters by adding contextual information directly in the UI.

items.title: Enhance Cost Rules with Localized Remarks in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an optional localized `remark` field to API and service cost rules, allowing for clearer explanations of model variants on the pricing page. The implementation leverages existing localization pipelines to support translations in 18 languages without affecting billing processes. Additionally, new tests have been added to ensure the functionality works as intended.

items.title: Streamline Action Buttons in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: The Maestro task result card now features action buttons for download, remix, and view code aligned in a single flex row, enhancing user experience. A new download icon has been added to the button, and adjustments ensure that multi-variant tasks display distinct download buttons. This update improves the visual layout and functionality of the task cards.

items.title: Enhance video rendering quality with new guards in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces a preflight guard to reject near-black video renders, ensuring only valid content is processed. Additionally, new skill authoring rules have been established to improve image framing, visibility, and text layout, preventing common rendering issues. The changes enhance the overall quality and reliability of video outputs in the Platform Service.

items.title: Enhance model card persistence in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures that expanded model cards remain visible after refreshing the model list in the admin console. Previously, the active card would collapse unexpectedly, impacting the reliability of the history action. Now, only existing keys are preserved, improving user experience during model interactions.

items.title: Enhance Admin Console with Upstream Checker History in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces read-only checker history support for the upstream admin console, allowing operators to access recent raw checker events. Key changes include a new API endpoint for fetching checker events and improved status filtering to include degraded-but-active models. These enhancements will aid in debugging provider/model failures more effectively.

items.title: Enhance Console UI with Compact Cards and Checker History in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the production UI for the admin console by making model cards more compact and adding a checker history feature. Key changes include the introduction of compact status dots, clearer status filters, and a new drawer for checker history per provider and model lane. Additionally, local updates for ranking and toggling checks enhance user experience without refreshing the entire model list.
